# Verify that git diff shows plaintext JSON diffs for the sesam audit log.

exec git init
exec git config user.email test@sesam.dev
exec git config user.name 'Sesam Test'

# Init sesam and commit the baseline audit log.
exec tree -a
exec sesam init --user admin
exec git add -A
exec git commit -m 'init sesam'

# Modify a secret and re-seal. This appends a new entry to the audit log.
cp modified_readme.md README.md
exec sesam seal

# Sanity-check: the audit log is modified, textconv is configured.
exec git status --short
stdout '.sesam/audit/log.jsonl'
exec git config --local diff.sesam-diff.textconv
stdout 'sesam show'
exec git check-attr diff -- .sesam/audit/log.jsonl
stdout 'sesam-diff'

# sesam show decrypts and outputs the current log as JSON.
exec sesam show .sesam/audit/log.jsonl
stdout '"operation"'

# Without textconv, git shows raw encrypted (base64) lines, not decrypted JSON.
exec git diff HEAD --no-textconv -- .sesam/audit/log.jsonl
! stdout '"operation"'

# git diff HEAD shows a JSON diff: the new seal entry appears as added lines.
exec git diff HEAD -- .sesam/audit/log.jsonl
stdout '"operation"'
stdout '"seal"'

-- modified_readme.md --
# modified readme

This is the new content after the change.
